Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenae
Off Calendar Nov-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SARAH BEYELER, JONATHAN BEYELER, ROCHELLE BEYELER'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAE. Appearances: Alexander Volchegursky of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP (392.2560) for defendants and moving parties; Gerald N. Shelley (714.557.3070) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: deny without prejudice to defendants seeking a protective order limiting who may see the information and for what purpose. Motion fails to attach required Separate Statement pursuant to CRC 3.1345(a)(5). On the merits, defendants have not established why records sought are covered by the right of privacy. Even assuming a right to privacy does exist for the information sought, plaintiff has overcome the burden by showing that information sought is directly relevant and essential to proving her claim. The request is narrowly tailored. The public interest in obtaining the discovery outweighs any claimed right to privacy. Pursuant to CCP 1987.2, grant request for award of plaintiff's reasonable expenses incurred in opposing the motion, which was made without substantial justification, in the amount of $2600 (8 hours x $325 hourly attorney fee charged by plaintiff's counsel). Grant request to impose this amount jointly and severally between and among defendants and their counsel. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Reporter: Loretta Najera CSR#5153

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gerald N. Shelley (714.557.3070) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party; Milla L. Lvovich of Lvovich & Volchegursky LLP (392.2560) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS SARAH BEYELER, JONATHAN BEYELER, ROCHELLE BEYELER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action for concealment. Plaintiff must allege a duty to disclose a fact to plaintiff or a fiduciary relationship, and defendants' intent to defraud. Demurrer overruled as to remaining causes of action. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
